sourceMovie.title: The Odyssey
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Odyssey for the same reason it broke through in 2016: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jérôme Salle keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Poseidon Adventure
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Odyssey worked for you, The Poseidon Adventure (1972) is a strong next pick. The two films share adventure and drama throughlines and momentum and clean stakes, even though the stories go in different directions.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Odyssey?
faq.answer: Try The Poseidon Adventure, Jeremiah Johnson, Remi, Nobody's Boy—each shares DNA with The Odyssey while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Odyssey?
faq.answer: The Odyssey (2016) is best described as Adventure, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Odyssey worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
